meaning: n.绂忓唸(鏃ユ湰涔濆窞鍖楅儴娓競)
examples: He was born in Fukuoka, Japan, where his parents still live.
The area on the north coast around the island's biggest city, Fukuoka, is abuzz.
Japanese designers Case-Real have completed the interior of a pastry shop and tearoom in Fukuoka, Japan.
The ambitious TheaterWorks project premiered in Tokyo on September 9 and will tour Osaka and Fukuoka.
This was the second time Japan had hosted the festival, the first being in Fukuoka in 2002.
